_comment: Deterministic tie-breaker rules for multi-agent review conflicts.
_usage: When two agents disagree on severity or action, apply the highest-priority matching rule.

rules.description: Security agent findings of critical or high severity are never downgraded by other agents.
rules.action: preserve — do not downgrade, do not suppress
rules.rationale: Security concerns in regulated environments are non-negotiable regardless of style or architecture preferences.

rules.description: When clean-code and logic agents conflict on the same finding, logic wins.
rules.action: use logic agent's severity and suggestion
rules.rationale: A logic error that happens to be readable is still a logic error. Style concerns are secondary.

rules.description: When performance and architecture agents conflict, defer to architecture for design decisions, performance for measurable regressions.
rules.action: if finding includes a measurable metric (query count, latency), use performance; otherwise use architecture
rules.rationale: Premature optimization is architecture's domain; confirmed regression is performance's domain.

false_positive_budget.retune_trigger: If any agent's false-positive rate exceeds 20% over a two-week period, review and narrow its charter.
false_positive_budget.measurement: Track findings marked 'false positive' by the human reviewer in each PR.
